regex-tester.whatItIs: The Regex Tester validates and debugs regular expressions with real-time matching, highlighting, and explanation. Essential for developers working with pattern matching, data validation, and text processing who need to test regex syntax quickly.

howToUse.name: How to Use the Regex Tester

steps.name: Enter regex pattern
steps.text: Type your regular expression pattern in the regex field. The tool supports JavaScript, Python, PCRE, and other common regex flavors.

steps.name: Enter test text
steps.text: Paste the text you want to test the regex against. Matches are highlighted in real-time as you type.

steps.name: Analyze matches
steps.text: Review highlighted matches, capture groups, and match positions. Use the explanation feature to understand each part of your regex.

steps.name: Generate code
steps.text: Copy the regex with code snippets for your programming language. Use replace mode to test substitution patterns.

faqs.question: What regex flavors are supported?
faqs.answer: The tool supports JavaScript (ECMAScript), Python (re module), PCRE (PHP), and common regex syntax used in most programming languages.

faqs.question: Can I test regex replacements?
faqs.answer: Yes, the replace mode lets you test substitution patterns. Enter a replacement string and see the transformed output in real-time.

faqs.question: How do I understand my regex?
faqs.answer: The explanation feature breaks down your regex into components, explaining what each part matches. This is helpful for learning and debugging complex patterns.

faqs.question: Is the Regex Tester free?
faqs.answer: Yes, testing and debugging regular expressions is completely free with unlimited usage.

regex-tester.seoDescription: Test regular expressions online for free. Debug regex patterns with real-time matching and highlighting. Support for JavaScript, Python, and PCRE flavors.

jwt-decoder.whatItIs: The JWT Decoder decodes JSON Web Tokens to inspect headers, payload, and signature without verification. Essential for debugging authentication flows and understanding token contents in web applications.
features: Copy decoded data with one click

howToUse.name: How to Use the JWT Decoder

steps.name: Paste JWT token
steps.text: Copy and paste your JWT token (the three-part string separated by dots) into the input field. No verification is required for decoding.

steps.name: Decode the token
steps.text: Click decode to parse the JWT. The tool splits the token into header, payload, and signature sections.

steps.name: Inspect the data
steps.text: Review the decoded header and payload in readable JSON format. Check claims like user ID, expiration, issuer, and custom data.

steps.name: Copy or debug
steps.text: Copy the decoded data for debugging or documentation. Use the information to troubleshoot authentication issues.

faqs.question: What is a JWT?
faqs.answer: JWT (JSON Web Token) is a compact, URL-safe token format used for securely transmitting information between parties. It consists of a header, payload, and signature separated by dots.

faqs.question: Does this verify the token signature?
faqs.answer: This tool decodes the token without verifying the signature. For signature verification, you need the secret key or public key used to sign the token.

faqs.question: Can I decode encrypted JWTs?
faqs.answer: Basic decoding works for unencrypted (JWS) tokens. Encrypted (JWE) tokens require decryption keys which this tool does not handle.

faqs.question: Is the JWT Decoder free?
faqs.answer: Yes, decoding JWT tokens is completely free with unlimited usage.

jwt-decoder.seoDescription: Decode JWT tokens online for free. Inspect JSON Web Token headers and payloads without verification. Debug authentication and API tokens.

uuid-v4-generator.whatItIs: The UUID v4 Generator creates random UUID (Universally Unique Identifier) version 4 strings for use in databases, APIs, and distributed systems. Generate cryptographically strong unique IDs for applications.
features: Support for standard and uppercase formats

howToUse.name: How to Use the UUID v4 Generator

steps.name: Select UUID options
steps.text: Choose the number of UUIDs to generate and the output format: standard lowercase, uppercase, or with braces.

steps.name: Generate UUIDs
steps.text: Click generate to create cryptographically secure UUID v4 values. Each UUID is guaranteed to be unique with extremely low collision probability.

steps.name: Review UUIDs
steps.text: See the generated UUIDs with their version and variant information. Verify the format meets your requirements.

steps.name: Copy and use
steps.text: Copy individual UUIDs or all at once. Use them as unique identifiers in databases, API responses, or distributed systems.

faqs.question: What is UUID v4?
faqs.answer: UUID v4 is a randomly generated 128-bit identifier. It uses random or pseudo-random numbers and has an extremely low probability of collision, making it suitable for unique IDs.

faqs.question: How unique are UUIDs?
faqs.answer: UUID v4 has 122 random bits, giving 5.3 x 10^36 possible values. The probability of collision is negligible for all practical purposes.

faqs.question: Can I generate multiple UUIDs at once?
faqs.answer: Yes, you can generate up to 1000 UUIDs in a single batch operation. Each UUID is independently generated with secure randomness.

faqs.question: Is the UUID v4 Generator free?
faqs.answer: Yes, generating UUIDs is completely free with unlimited usage.

uuid-v4-generator.seoDescription: Generate UUID v4 online for free. Create cryptographically secure random UUIDs for databases, APIs, and applications. Batch generation supported.

nanoid-generator.whatItIs: The NanoID Generator creates compact, URL-friendly unique identifiers using the NanoID algorithm. Shorter and more secure than UUIDs, perfect for database keys, URL shorteners, and file naming.

howToUse.name: How to Use the NanoID Generator

steps.name: Set ID length
steps.text: Choose the length of your NanoID. Default is 21 characters, which provides sufficient uniqueness for most applications.

steps.name: Select character set
steps.text: Choose the character set: URL-safe (default), alphanumeric, or custom. URL-safe excludes similar-looking characters like I, l, and 1.

steps.name: Generate NanoIDs
steps.text: Click generate to create NanoID strings. The tool uses a cryptographically secure random generator.

steps.name: Copy and use
steps.text: Copy the generated NanoID for use as a database key, URL slug, file name, or any unique identifier requirement.

faqs.question: What is NanoID?
faqs.answer: NanoID is a tiny, secure, URL-friendly unique string ID generator. It produces shorter IDs than UUIDs with better collision resistance and URL safety.

faqs.question: NanoID vs UUID: which is better?
faqs.answer: NanoIDs are shorter, URL-safe, and more customizable than UUIDs. Use NanoIDs for URLs and database keys, UUIDs for distributed system IDs where length doesn't matter.

faqs.question: How long should my NanoID be?
faqs.answer: 21 characters is the default and works for most use cases. For higher security or larger datasets, use 32+ characters. Each additional character exponentially increases uniqueness.

faqs.question: Is the NanoID Generator free?
faqs.answer: Yes, generating NanoIDs is completely free with unlimited usage.

nanoid-generator.seoDescription: Generate NanoID online for free. Create compact, URL-friendly unique identifiers. Secure alternative to UUIDs for database keys and URLs.
